paper_title: When all directed cycles have length three
summary: Conjecture 1.2 asks whether a strongly 2-connected digraph drawn (with crossings) in an annulus so that every directed cycle has winding number one can always be redrawn without crossings while preserving that winding-number condition. The author explicitly states 'we cannot prove it.' No verified follow-up paper resolving or refuting the conjecture was found in the literature as of May 2026; a co-authored paper by Seymour, Berger, and Carter titled 'When all directed cycles have the same weight' is listed as forthcoming but does not appear to address this conjecture directly.
search_queries: Seymour "2410.13008" digraph annulus winding number conjecture
Daniel Carter digraph annulus winding number counterexample Seymour conjecture
notes: The AI-generated summary from one search result claimed the conjecture was disproved by Daniel Carter, but direct WebFetch of the arXiv HTML (arxiv.org/html/2410.13008) found no mention of Carter and no counterexample — that claim is unverifiable and likely a hallucination. Seymour's publication page lists a forthcoming paper with Berger and Carter titled 'When all directed cycles have the same weight,' which is thematically related but distinct. No follow-up found after 5 web calls; status is open with high confidence given the conjecture is less than 2 years old.
